title: Image creation fails during file deletion with "Too many links" on BTRFS as underlying file system
body:
The problem occurred during image creation with a base image (24 GB) that contained many log files (2807 files in 187 directories) which had to be removed during the build process.

The system was Debian 11 Bullseye with Podman from the Debian (3.0.1). After the issue was detected and could not be resolved the system was migrated to Fedora Server 34 with
```
podman version
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Mon Aug 30 20:46:36 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

The problem persisted on Fedora. In a second approach to solve the issue the BTRFS file system was replaced with Ext4. This fixed the issue.

```
rm: cannot remove '/path/to/log/E037A1590E4C1755F442FB1.log': Too many links
rm: cannot remove '/path/to/log/E865077F126553F9384BC3B.log': Too many links
rm: cannot remove '/path/to/log/E9B59293B9B55E6B9702255.log': Too many links
rm: cannot remove '/path/to/log/EC5BBBDEBD3D1A8CD82DEE7.log': Too many links
rm: cannot remove '/path/to/log/F1D1E7A9D433E3B559C4331.log': Too many links
rm: cannot remove '/path/to/log/F441B3DAEB2D893F5BF021C.log': Too many links
rm: cannot remove '/path/to/log/F51678E1BB7194F1AF7EF0F.log': Too many links
...
```

comments.body:
@veita Could this be a `btrfs` issue or more like the contents in your image hitting limits ? I can see similar issues are opened for docker pull as well over coreos. I think BTRFS hard links limit for a directory is too small i.e 256 hard links not sure. https://github.com/coreos/bugs/issues/474

But still i am really unsure if this is a buildah issue.
@veita Could you also please paste output of `podman info` and paste it here, also please let me know if its using `overlay` or `btrfs` driver.

> @veita Could this be a `btrfs`
> [...]
> But still i am really unsure if this is a buildah issue.

I also think this might be an BTRFS rather than a Buildah issue.

> @veita Could you also please paste output of `podman info` and paste it here, also please let me know if its using `overlay` or `btrfs` driver.

Unfortunately I cannot supply this information since we have migrated the system from Debian 11 Bullseye to Fedora Server 34, so it doesn't exist anymore. But it was Podman from the official Debian repository, so it should have been 3.0.1.

Closing this in light of https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3554#issuecomment-934211787 and https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3554#issuecomment-934288342 . Please feel free to reopen if you see this again.
